skill.name: ui-design
skill.description: Guides structured UI styling changes with screenshots, current-state diagrams, option selection, focused code edits, and small refinements.
skill.summary: Coordinate UI layout and styling edits through visual confirmation and scoped implementation steps.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown code fences, front matter, and non-ASCII content as possible command execution, weak cryptography, and obfuscation. Review of SKILL.md found only workflow guidance, ASCII layout examples, and no executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic logic, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Flagged As Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md line 24 is a markdown code fence for an ASCII UI example, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable command or user-controlled command string is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines show a fenced documentation example containing box drawing text. There is no Ruby code, shell command, or execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Closing Fence Flagged As Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md line 31 is the closing delimiter of a markdown code block. It does not execute anything and is part of documentation formatting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 31 is only a markdown fence delimiter. It has no command name, arguments, interpreter, or runtime behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Second Markdown Code Fence Flagged As Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md line 41 opens another markdown code block for UI layout options. The content is an ASCII diagram and contains no shell execution behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code block is a visual layout example inside prose instructions. No executable language or external command call appears in the block.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Front Matter Name Flagged As Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md line 2 points to the skill name in YAML front matter. It does not reference MD5, SHA1, DES, or any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 2 contains only the metadata value name: ui-design. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive operation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Flagged As Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md line 3 points to a Chinese-language description of the UI collaboration workflow. No cryptographic algorithm or security function is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural-language metadata describing UI style edits. I found no crypto terms, APIs, encoded payloads, or algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Front Matter Flagged As Obfuscation
low_findings.description: The static finding at SKILL.md line 1 identifies normal YAML front matter. The file is readable markdown documentation, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is human-readable markdown with Chinese prose and ASCII diagrams. High entropy likely came from multilingual text and box drawing characters, not hidden payloads.

content.value_statement: UI styling requests often fail when the target area and desired result are unclear. This skill creates a screenshot-led workflow for confirming the current state, comparing options, and applying focused changes.
actual_capabilities: Asks for screenshots or visual references before changing UI styling.
Describes the current layout with ASCII diagrams for confirmation.
Presents two or three alternative layout plans before implementation.
Keeps code edits limited to the selected UI change.
Handles small follow-up refinements without restarting the full workflow.
limitations: Does not provide a standalone design system or component library.
Requires user feedback when screenshots or goals are ambiguous.
Does not replace visual QA in a browser or device test environment.
Focuses on layout and styling, not backend behavior or product strategy.

use_cases.title: Triage Layout Feedback
use_cases.description: Convert a vague UI complaint into a confirmed current-state diagram and a small set of layout options.

use_cases.title: Review Design Alternatives
use_cases.description: Compare two or three visual arrangements before asking the AI to edit code.

use_cases.title: Control Scope During UI Polish
use_cases.description: Keep styling changes focused on the selected element and avoid unrelated refactors.

prompt_templates.title: Confirm Current UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ui-design workflow for this screen. First read the relevant files and describe the current layout with an ASCII diagram before suggesting changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a visual change

prompt_templates.title: Compare Layout Options
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to improve the spacing and placement in this area. Give me two or three ASCII layout options with one short reason for each.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Selected Option
prompt_templates.prompt: Use option B from your layout proposals. Make the smallest code change needed and leave unrelated structure unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Refine After Review
prompt_templates.prompt: The selected layout is close. Make only this refinement: increase the spacing between the title and controls, then summarize the exact files changed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced follow-up polish after a first pass

output_examples.input: The header tools look awkward. Help me fix the layout.
output_examples.output: The assistant first maps the current header with an ASCII diagram, then offers two or three revised arrangements for the tools.

output_examples.input: Use the option where search and refresh are on the same row.
output_examples.output: The assistant applies a focused UI edit for that selected arrangement and avoids unrelated cleanup.

output_examples.input: The color is too strong after the change.
output_examples.output: The assistant treats this as a small refinement and directly adjusts the color without reopening the full option process.

best_practices: Provide a screenshot or a precise file and component name when starting.
Ask for ASCII layout options before approving code changes.
Use short, specific feedback for refinements after the first edit.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the assistant to guess the desired layout from a vague request.
Do not combine visual polish with unrelated refactors in one request.
Do not skip browser review when the final spacing or alignment matters.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure UI styling changes so the assistant confirms the current layout, proposes options, and edits only the chosen design.

faq.question: Does it generate finished visual designs?
faq.answer: No. It guides collaboration around styling and layout changes in an existing interface.

faq.question: Why does it use ASCII diagrams?
faq.answer: ASCII diagrams make layout intent explicit before code changes begin.

faq.question: Can it work without screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes, if the relevant code and target area are clear. Screenshots improve accuracy for visual issues.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation only and no executable command, network, or secret-handling behavior.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
